sections.html: Stuck on a lab? Confused about a concept? Here’s how to get help. Try them in order—most questions get answered quickly through Discord.

sections.label: 1. Ask on Discord (Fastest Response)
paragraphs: The class Discord is the fastest way to get help. Post your question in #lab-help or #course-questions and classmates or the instructor will respond—often within minutes.
items: Describe what you’re trying to do
Share the error message (use code blocks!)
Be specific—“it doesn’t work” doesn’t help us help you

sections.label: 2. Book an Appointment (One-on-One Help)
paragraphs: Need dedicated one-on-one time? Book a 15-minute appointment through Calendly. Great for complex debugging, career questions, or anything that needs focused attention.

sections.label: 3. Email the Instructor (Private Matters Only)
paragraphs: Email is best for private matters: grade questions, accommodations, personal circumstances, or anything you don’t want to discuss publicly.
sections.tip: Pro tip: Include “IS3513” in the subject line so I can find your email quickly.

sections.label: Please Don’t:
sections.html: DM the instructor on Discord for questions that should be public (others probably have the same question). Don’t wait until the night before a deadline to ask for help. Don’t email technical questions—Discord gets faster responses.
No weekly Zoom — this is an asynchronous course. Discord and Calendly cover 99% of what you need.
